MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Mar-06-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. TYRONE MATTHEWS, Esq. of Matthews Law Group, P.C. (tel:855-303-7771) for Plaintiff.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: RESTORATION SPECIALISTS, INC. D/B/A DRY1 OUT's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT / DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND.

Matter discussed. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' motion to set aside default judgment is granted.

It is an ethical violation not to warn opposing counsel before requesting entry of default, and plaintiff's counsel does not claim he did so. (Lasalle v. Vogel (2019) 36 Cal.App.5th 127, 135; Matthews Dec. 9:4-9.)

On the other hand, defendants and their counsel were mistaken in not being more attentive to the matter. (CCP 473(b).) It is the California courts' strong policy to adjudicate disputes on their merits.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
